questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A small ball of mass $m$ is released from rest from a height $h$ above the liquid surface. The density of ball is $\frac{\rho}{3}:$ (assume no splash)
options: If $h \geq H$, then the ball will be able to enter the liquid of higher density
If $\mathrm{h} \geq \frac{7 \mathrm{H}}{2}$, then the ball will be able to strike the bottom of the vessel
If $\mathrm{h}=2 \mathrm{H}$, then the ball will cross the interface of two liquids with kinetic energy $=\mathrm{mgH}$
If $\mathrm{h}=0$ then ball will float on the surface
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & m g\left(h+\frac{H}{2}\right)=[v \rho g] \frac{H}{2} \\ & h=H \\ & m g\left(h+\frac{H}{2}+\frac{H}{2}\right)=[v \rho g] \frac{H}{2}+[v 2 \rho g] \frac{H}{2} \\ & h=\frac{7 H}{2} \\ & m g\left(h+\frac{H}{2}\right)-[v p g] \frac{H}{2}=\text { K.E. } \\ & m g\left(\frac{5 H}{2}\right)-\frac{3}{2} m g H=\text { K.E. } \\ & \text { K.E. }=m g H . .\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A rectangular vessel of dimension $(l \times b \times h)$ and mass $M$ contains a liquid of density $\rho$. The vessel has an orifice at its bottom at a distance $c$ from the rear wall as shown in the figure
options: The maximum volume of the water that can be stored when the vessel is accelerated is hcb/2
The maximum volume of the water that can be stored when the vessel is accelerated is hb/2
Force $F$ that must be applied when maximum water is stored is $\left[M+\frac{h c b \rho}{2}\right] \frac{h g}{c}$
Force $F$ that must be applied when maximum water is stored is $\left[M+\frac{h c b p}{2}\right] \frac{l g}{c}$
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{A}, \mathrm{C}$ - The maximum amount of water that can be retained is shown in the figure. If $\theta$ is the angle made by the water surface with the horizontal, then
$\tan \theta=\frac{h}{c}=\frac{a}{g} \quad \Rightarrow a=\frac{h g}{c}$ So the maximum volume that can be retained is $(1 / 2) \times h \times c \times b$ and $F=\left[M+\frac{h c b \rho}{2}\right] \frac{h g}{c}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: A cylindrical vessel of 90 cm height is kept filled upto the brim. It has four holes $1,2,3$, 4 which are respectively at heights of $20 \mathrm{~cm}, 30 \mathrm{~cm}, 40 \mathrm{~cm}$ and 50 cm from the horizontal floor PQ. The water falling at the maximum horizontal distance from the vessel comes from
options: Hole number 4
Hole number 3
Hole number 2
Hole number 1
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: An iron block and a wooden block are positioned in a vessel containing water as shown in the figure. The iron block (1) hangs from a massless string with a rigid support from the top while the wooden block floats being tied to the bottom thorugh a massless string. Now, if the vessel starts accelerating upwards
options: Tension in the string 1 will decrease
Tension in the string 1 will increase
Tension in string 2 will decrease
Tension in the string 2 will increase
questions.solution: Since $\mathrm{g}_{\text {eff }}$ increases tenstion in both strings will increase.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A light cylindrical vessel of radius $r$ is kept on a rough horizontal surface so that it cannot slide but can topple. It is filled with water upto a height ' $2 h$ ' and a small hole of area ' $a$ ' is punched in its wall so that the water coming out of it falls at the maxmum distance from a vertical plane through the hole, along horizontal surface. Water comes out horizontally from the hole. The value of $h$ for which the cylinder topples is/ are
options: $\frac{\pi r^3}{2 a}$
$\frac{2 \pi r^3}{a}$
$\frac{3 \pi r^3}{2 a}$
$\frac{4 \pi r^3}{3 a}$
questions.solution:
For toppling, $\quad \rho a v^2 \times h \geq \pi r^2 2 h \rho g \times r$
$\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{P} \rho a 2 g h \times h \geq \pi r^2 2 h \rho g r \\ & \mathrm{P} h \geq \frac{\pi r^3}{a}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A hollow cylinder of radius R and height $\mathrm{h}(\ll \mathrm{R})$ is completely filled with a liquid having coefficient of viscosity $\eta$. Now a cylinder of radius $R$ is placed over it and is rotated with constant angular velocity $\omega$ by an external agent as shown.
options: Net viscous force acting on the cylinder is zero.
external torque required to rotate the cylinder with constant angular velocity $\omega$ is $\frac{\pi \omega R^4 \eta}{h}$.
external torque required to rotate the cylinder with constant angular velocity $\omega$ is $\frac{\pi \omega R^4 \eta}{2 h}$.
external power required to rotate the cylinder with constant angular velocity $\omega$ is $\frac{\pi \omega^2 R^4 \eta}{h}$.
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & d \tau=\left[\frac{\omega x}{h} \eta 2 \pi x d x\right] x=\frac{2 \pi \omega \eta}{h} x^3 d x \\ & \therefore \tau=\int d \tau=\frac{\pi \omega R^4 \eta}{2 h} \Rightarrow P=\vec{\tau} \cdot \vec{\omega}=\frac{\pi \omega^2 R^4 \eta}{2 h}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Two ice cubes of side 10 cm , having cavity of volume $20 \mathrm{~cm}^3$ at centre of cube but filled with different materials A and B respectively. The specific gravity of material A is 4.9 and specific gravity of material $B$ is 1.9. Now these cubes are placed in two different vessels of same base area as shown in figure. The water level before putting blocks in vessels are same. Assume that ice melts uniformly from all sides and with same constant rate in both the vessels. (specific gravity of ice $=0.9$ ). In (C) and (D) graph showing the variation of heights of water-level in two vessels with time. Choose the correct statement :
options: the ratio of initial submerged volumes of the blocks containing A and B is $\frac{49}{46}$
the ratio of initial submerged volumes of the blocks containing $A$ and $B$ is $49 / 19$
questions.solution:
$\rho V_A g=\left(m_{i c e}+m_A\right) g$ and $\rho V_B g=\left(m_{i c e}+m_B\right) g \Rightarrow \frac{V_A}{V_B}=\frac{m_{i c e}+m_A}{m_{i c e}+m_B}=\frac{0.9 \times 980+4.9 \times 20}{0.9 \times 980+1.9 \times 20}=\frac{49}{46}$
Also A will start sinking first.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: n drops of a liquid, each with surface energy E , joining to form a single drop
options: some energy will be released in the process
some energy will be absorbed in the process
the energy released or absorbed will be $\mathrm{E}\left(\mathrm{n}-\mathrm{n}^{2 / 3}\right)$
the energy released or absorbed will be $\mathrm{nE}\left(2^{2 / 3}-1\right)$
questions.solution:
$$
\mathrm{E}=\mathrm{T} 4 \pi \mathrm{r}^2 \Rightarrow \frac{4}{3} \pi \mathrm{R}^3=\mathrm{n} \times \frac{4 \pi}{3} \mathrm{r}^3 \Rightarrow \mathrm{n}=\frac{\mathrm{R}^3}{\mathrm{r}^3} \Rightarrow \mathrm{R}=\mathrm{n}^{1 / 3} \mathrm{r}
$$

Surface energy of big drop $E^{\prime}=T 4 \pi R^2=T 4 \pi n^{2 / 3} r^2=E^{2 / 3}$
Energy released $=\mathrm{nE}-\mathrm{E}^{\prime}=\mathrm{nE}-\mathrm{n}^{2 / 3} \mathrm{E}=\mathrm{E}\left(\mathrm{n}-\mathrm{n}^{2 / 3}\right)$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A cubical block is floating in a liquid with one third of it's volume immersed in the liquid. When the whole system accelerates upwards with acceleration of $\mathrm{g} / 2$.
options: The fraction of volume immersed in the liquid will change.
The buoyancy force on the block will change.
The buoyancy force will increase by 50 percent.
The pressure in the liquid will increased.
questions.solution:
If the volume immersed initially is $(V / 3)$. Then $\frac{V}{3} \rho g=m g$....(i)
If the volume immersed when the system accelerates is $\mathrm{V}^{\prime}$ then
$V^{\prime} \rho\left(g+\frac{g}{2}\right)-m g=\frac{m g}{2} \Rightarrow V^{\prime}=\frac{V}{3}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
A vertical capillary tube with inside radius 0.25 mm is submerged into water so that the length of its part protruding over the water surface is equal to 25 mm . surface tension of water is $73 \times 10^{-3}$ $\mathrm{N} / \mathrm{m}$ and angle of contact is zero degree for glass and water, acceleration due to gravity is 9.8 $\mathrm{m} / \mathrm{s}^2$. Then choose correct statement.
where R is radius of meniscus and h is height of water in capillary tube.
options: h = 25 mm
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & \text { Capillary height } \mathrm{h}=\frac{2 \mathrm{~T} \cos \theta}{\mathrm{r} \rho \mathrm{g}} \\ & \Rightarrow \mathrm{h}=59.6 \mathrm{~mm}\end{aligned}$
here 59.6 mm is greater than the protruding part hence water will rise in the capillary of insufficient height 25 mm .
Now, $R=\frac{2 T}{\mathrm{hrg}}=0.6 \mathrm{~mm}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
A vertical capillary tube with inside radius 0.25 mm is submerged into water so that the length of its part protruding over the water surface is equal to 25 mm . surface tension of water is $73 \times 10^{-3}$ $\mathrm{N} / \mathrm{m}$ and angle of contact is zero degree for glass and water, acceleration due to gravity is 9.8 $\mathrm{m} / \mathrm{s}^2$. Then choose correct statement.
(where R is radius of meniscus and h is height of water in capillary tube.)
options: $R=0.25 \mathrm{~mm}$
$\mathrm{h}=59.6 \mathrm{~mm}$
$R=0.60 \mathrm{~mm}$
$\mathrm{h}=25 \mathrm{~mm}$
questions.solution:
Capillary height $h=\frac{2 \mathrm{~T} \cos \theta}{\mathrm{r} \rho \mathrm{g}} \Rightarrow \mathrm{h}=59.6 \mathrm{~mm}$
here 59.6 mm is greater than the protruding part hence water will rise in the capillary of insufficient height 25 mm .
Now, $R=\frac{2 T}{\mathrm{hrg}}=0.6 \mathrm{~mm}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

Buoyant force decreases as lift accelerates up
